Cash Flow.value: Total Payment to Agency and Producer

Data.value: Largest outdoor stadium in Singapore; hosts major concerts & sports. (IQ Magazine)
Indoor concert arena near National Stadium. (Wikipedia)
Major football venue; also suitable for large events. (StadiumDB)
Big stadium in Tokyo area used for sports and large concerts. (StadiumDB)
Large dome frequently used for concerts and events. (IQ Magazine)
Hong Kong’s largest outdoor stadium for big events. (IQ Magazine)
Largest indoor concert venue in Hong Kong. (Wikipedia)
Largest football stadium that can host major concerts. (Wikipedia)
Slightly larger than World Cup Stadium; multi-purpose venue. (Wikipedia)
Major stadium in Busan suitable for large events. (Wikipedia)
Another large World Cup venue in Korea. (Wikipedia)
Baseball dome used for concerts (~25k config). (Wikipedia)
Big stadium/pop star shows in Singapore often fall in this range; big acts like BLACKPINK had prices from ~S$168 to S$428. Average can sit around S$200 (~$150). (Klook Travel)
For major tours in domes/stadiums, Japan often has slightly lower face-value prices than Western markets; standard seats in recent big tours (e.g., BTS/J-POP) start ~¥11,000–¥15,000. (Rebel Music)
Coldplay stadium pricing shows starting tiers ~HK$699–HK$999, with many mid tickets ~HK$1,299–HK$1,699; an A-list pop act would average near HK$1,200. (Klook Travel)
Big concerts domestically (K-pop) often have standard tiers around ₩150k–₩250k (~$110–$180), and major Western tours can be in similar ranges. (koreatimes.co.kr)
